sn35.229:1.1: “‘The ocean, the ocean,’ says the uninstructed run-of-the-mill person.
sn35.229:1.2: But that’s not the ocean in the discipline of the noble ones.
sn35.229:1.3: It’s a great mass of water, a great body of water.
sn35.229:1.4: “There are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.229:1.5: These are called the ocean in the discipline of the noble ones.
sn35.229:1.6: Here this cosmos—with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk—is, for the most part, submerged, like a tangled skein, a knotted ball of string, like matted rushes & reeds, and doesn’t go beyond the plane of deprivation, the bad destination, the lower realm, the wandering-on. “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… aromas cognizable via the nose…
sn35.229:2.1: flavors cognizable via the tongue… tactile sensations cognizable via the body… “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.229:2.2: These are called the ocean in the discipline of the noble ones.
sn35.229:2.3: Here this cosmos—with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk—is, for the most part, submerged, like a tangled skein, a knotted ball of string, like matted rushes & reeds, and doesn’t go beyond the plane of deprivation, the bad destination, the lower realm, the wandering-on.
sn35.229:3.3: crosses over this ocean, with its danger of sharks, demons,
sn35.229:3.4: waves, so very hard to cross.
sn35.229:4.1: He’s one who has overcome ties, forsaken death,
sn35.229:4.2: abandoned suffering & stress, is acquisition-free with no further becoming.
sn35.229:4.3: Having gone to his end, he has no measure,
sn35.229:4.4: has bewildered, I tell you, Death’s king.
